title: HTTP/1.1 Response Serialization, Field Order, Framing, and Writes · Go Build Room
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first canonical bounded HTTP/1.1 response serializer for http/1.1 response serialization, field order, framing, and writes. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of canonical bounded HTTP/1.1 response serializer while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order.
Reject CR, LF, NUL, controls, invalid names, and unsafe values before serializing any response field.
Choose no-content, fixed-length, chunked, or close-delimited output from method, status, known length, streaming, version, and reuse policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Response Commit Order
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 8902 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to select one output framing mode while retaining partial-write and field-validation evidence; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order.

What must be established before implementation?
